phases.name: Foundation — the at-a-glance Countered flag on the SCORING-BEARING GET /score per-contribution breakdown rows (US-CF-002, THICK) + the flatten-once wiring confirmations

steps.name: Wire the SCORE_COUNTER_LEGEND SSOT constant + the threaded &presence render chain (render_score_results_fragment → render_score_pairing → render_score_breakdown) + the score_counter_presence flatten-once helper into GET /score and return the htmx fragment flagging the countered contribution row beside its verbatim subtotal, legend present, subtotals summing to weight (THICK walking skeleton)
steps.description: The thick foundational walking-skeleton step (US-CF-002, SF-1). It forces the WHOLE /score at-a-glance flag thread into existence end-to-end and REUSES the slice-12 read VERBATIM (ADR-048 — NO new port method, NO new SQL, NO adapter-duckdb edit) + the slice-13 render_countered_link SSOT VERBATIM (NO new render fn). (viewer-domain) Add the NEW pub const SCORE_COUNTER_LEGEND anti-misread legend SSOT constant (blocklist-clean: never disputed/refuted/false/penalty/deduction/lowered/disputed score; ADR-051 §6.3). Thread presence: &HashSet<String> DOWN the render call chain alongside the UNCHANGED &ScoreState: render_score_results_fragment(state, presence) (the fragment fn BOTH shapes embed) → render_score_page(state, presence) wraps the SAME fragment in chrome → render_score_result(state, presence) renders the SCORE_COUNTER_LEGEND ONCE before the pairings ONLY in the Scored arm (Form/NoClaims render no legend, no flag) then iterates view.ranked calling render_score_pairing(pairing, presence) → render_score_breakdown(pairing, presence). render_score_breakdown renders the existing six cells VERBATIM (author/cid/confidence/author-bonus/triangulation-bonus/subtotal) THEN the additive render_countered_link(&contribution.cid.0, presence.contains(&contribution.cid.0)) BESIDE the verbatim subtotal — an un-countered contribution renders byte-identically to slice-09 (the REUSED link fn emits NOTHING when is_countered == false). The render becomes a TOTAL function of (ScoreState, presence): it NEVER re-orders/re-ranks/re-weights/re-groups/subtracts; it reads each number straight off the UNCHANGED WeightedPairing scoring::score produced; the presence set can ONLY gate the additive marker, NEVER reach a number (the sum-to-weight orthogonality, guaranteed by construction, ADR-051 §7). The scoring crate is UNTOUCHED (NO is_countered field on scoring::Contribution — the bool lives nowhere; presence is threaded, ADR-051 §4). (adapter-http-viewer) In resolve_score_state's Scored arm the SANDWICH gains ONE REUSED read + the flatten: feed = store.query_contributor_scoring_feed UNCHANGED, view = scoring::score UNCHANGED pure compute, then the NEW effect-shell helper presence = score_counter_presence(store, &view) which flattens view.ranked.iter().flat_map(|p| p.contributions()).map(|c| c.cid.0.clone()) into ONE Vec<String> and calls store.counter_presence_for(&cids).unwrap_or_default() ONCE across ALL pairings (degrade → empty set, no flags, never a 5xx), then render by Shape threading &presence (render_score_results_fragment for Fragment, render_score_page for FullPage). Form/NoClaims build NO view → score_counter_presence is never called → 0 queries; render with &HashSet::new() so the signature is uniform. NO new route, NO new crate (workspace stays 21), NO ports/adapter-duckdb/cli change (the existing DuckDbStoreReadAdapter already implements counter_presence_for; the existing startup store probe covers it — Earned-Trust: no new probe owed, architecture-design.md §Earned-Trust). Materialize the seed_score_breakdown_one_contribution_countered support seam (a scored contributor's multi-row breakdown via the production peer add + peer pull federation path + exactly one contribution countered by a DISTINCT peer's verifiable counter landing in peer_claim_references; recover the render-order CIDs → SeededScoreBreakdown with contributor_did + countered_cids + uncountered_cids + ordered_cids) + the assert_score_row_flagged_countered / assert_score_row_not_flagged / assert_score_legend_present_and_blocklist_clean / assert_score_html_breakdown_sums_to_weight_with_flag helper bodies in support/mod.rs. BUILD-BEFORE-RUN: the AT spawns the openlore (viewer) bin via ViewerServer::start, so the run MUST cargo build -p cli --bin openlore before executing these ATs. NO second binary (the presence read is a LOCAL read). xtask delta: NONE (ADR-051 §8 / component-boundaries.md — no new dep edge: viewer-domain → scoring already exists from slice-09; the REUSED ref-tables-only read does not trip no_cross_table_join_elides_author; the viewer capability boundary is unchanged).
criteria: GET /score?contributor=<did> with the HX-Request header, over a seeded local store where exactly one contribution of a scored contributor's multi-row breakdown (the one countered by a DISTINCT peer from seed_score_breakdown_one_contribution_countered) has a counter and the others do not, returns 200 text/html containing ONLY the #score-results fragment (no page chrome, no <head>): the countered contribution row carries the neutral 'Countered' marker rendered as a render-only <a href="/claims/{cid}"> one-hop link to that claim's slice-11 thread BESIDE its verbatim subtotal, and every un-countered contribution row carries NO marker.

The /score route reuses the slice-12 StoreReadPort::counter_presence_for(&[String]) -> HashSet<String> batch read VERBATIM (no new port method, no new SQL) via the NEW score_counter_presence helper that flattens EVERY Contribution.cid across EVERY WeightedPairing into ONE call, reusing the slice-13 render_countered_link SSOT VERBATIM (no new render fn), holding NO signing key, adding no write/sign/counter route, and degrading a presence-read failure to an empty presence set (no flags) rather than a 5xx.

The breakdown carries the NEW SCORE_COUNTER_LEGEND anti-misread legend rendered ONCE (blocklist-clean — never disputed/refuted/false/penalty/deduction/lowered/disputed score), and the per-contribution subtotals STILL sum to the displayed pairing weight WITH the flag present (the countered contribution renders its FULL original subtotal — the counter subtracts nothing; the flag is SHOWN, never APPLIED).
acceptance_criteria: GET /score?contributor=<did> with the HX-Request header, over a seeded local store where exactly one contribution of a scored contributor's multi-row breakdown (the one countered by a DISTINCT peer from seed_score_breakdown_one_contribution_countered) has a counter and the others do not, returns 200 text/html containing ONLY the #score-results fragment (no page chrome, no <head>): the countered contribution row carries the neutral 'Countered' marker rendered as a render-only <a href="/claims/{cid}"> one-hop link to that claim's slice-11 thread BESIDE its verbatim subtotal, and every un-countered contribution row carries NO marker.

The /score route reuses the slice-12 StoreReadPort::counter_presence_for(&[String]) -> HashSet<String> batch read VERBATIM (no new port method, no new SQL) via the NEW score_counter_presence helper that flattens EVERY Contribution.cid across EVERY WeightedPairing into ONE call, reusing the slice-13 render_countered_link SSOT VERBATIM (no new render fn), holding NO signing key, adding no write/sign/counter route, and degrading a presence-read failure to an empty presence set (no flags) rather than a 5xx.

The breakdown carries the NEW SCORE_COUNTER_LEGEND anti-misread legend rendered ONCE (blocklist-clean — never disputed/refuted/false/penalty/deduction/lowered/disputed score), and the per-contribution subtotals STILL sum to the displayed pairing weight WITH the flag present (the countered contribution renders its FULL original subtotal — the counter subtracts nothing; the flag is SHOWN, never APPLIED).
steps.mutation_note: Adds the load-bearing pure-core surface (the threaded &presence render chain + render_score_breakdown's presence.contains(&cid) flag branch over the REUSED slice-12 read + the SCORE_COUNTER_LEGEND constant + the score_counter_presence flatten) over the REUSED slice-12 read + slice-13 render fn. Extend nightly mutation testing to this projection/render (presence.contains true/false flag branch, the un-countered byte-identity arm, the legend-once render site, the flatten that collects every Contribution.cid across every pairing, and that the presence bool never reaches a weight/subtotal/rank/order).

steps.name: A contribution countered by TWO distinct authors shows exactly ONE neutral presence marker on its /score row (anti-merging GOLD) and the marker is a render-only one-hop link to the thread (mostly confirmatory)
steps.description: The /score presence-only anti-merging GOLD + the one-hop drill-link (US-CF-002, SF-2; AC-SCORE-PRESENCE / AC-002-LINK). Largely confirmatory once 01-01's flag render exists — the DISTINCT collapse and the <a href> link shape already hold; this pins the neutral single-marker phrasing and the one-hop drill on the observable score surface. A contribution countered by TWO DISTINCT authors shows on its /score breakdown row EXACTLY ONE neutral 'Countered' marker (the DISTINCT referenced_cid collapses both counters to one membership in the HashSet → one flag), never 'countered by 2', never a count/verdict — the per-counter attribution lives in the slice-11 thread the marker LINKS to. The marker on the countered row is a render-only <a href="/claims/{cid}"> ONE-HOP link navigable without JS to the slice-11 detail thread. The flag is orthogonal to the score: the twice-countered contribution's subtotal, its pairing weight, and the pairing rank are unchanged (the subtotals still sum to the weight). Materializes seed_score_breakdown_target_two_counters_distinct_authors, assert_score_flag_is_single_neutral_presence, assert_score_flag_links_to_thread in support/mod.rs. Build-before-run as 01-01. xtask delta: NONE.
criteria: GET /score?contributor=<did> over a store where a contribution is countered by two DISTINCT authors (seed_score_breakdown_target_two_counters_distinct_authors) shows that contribution's breakdown row with EXACTLY ONE neutral 'Countered' marker, and the body carries NO count / 'countered by N' / verdict phrasing anywhere (presence-only, anti-merging; assert_score_flag_is_single_neutral_presence, AC-SCORE-PRESENCE).

The 'Countered' marker on the twice-countered /score row is a render-only <a href="/claims/{cid}"> one-hop anchor (navigation TEXT, never an executable control; assert_score_flag_links_to_thread) linking to the slice-11 thread where the two counters are individually attributed (AC-002-LINK).

The un-countered contribution rows carry NO marker and the subtotals STILL sum to the displayed pairing weight — the marker is orthogonal to the score (its subtotal, the pairing weight, and the pairing rank are unchanged; assert_score_html_breakdown_sums_to_weight_with_flag).
acceptance_criteria: GET /score?contributor=<did> over a store where a contribution is countered by two DISTINCT authors (seed_score_breakdown_target_two_counters_distinct_authors) shows that contribution's breakdown row with EXACTLY ONE neutral 'Countered' marker, and the body carries NO count / 'countered by N' / verdict phrasing anywhere (presence-only, anti-merging; assert_score_flag_is_single_neutral_presence, AC-SCORE-PRESENCE).

The 'Countered' marker on the twice-countered /score row is a render-only <a href="/claims/{cid}"> one-hop anchor (navigation TEXT, never an executable control; assert_score_flag_links_to_thread) linking to the slice-11 thread where the two counters are individually attributed (AC-002-LINK).

The un-countered contribution rows carry NO marker and the subtotals STILL sum to the displayed pairing weight — the marker is orthogonal to the score (its subtotal, the pairing weight, and the pairing rank are unchanged; assert_score_html_breakdown_sums_to_weight_with_flag).
steps.mutation_note: The single-neutral-presence render (one marker per countered contribution-cid, no count/verdict phrasing) and the render-only <a href="/claims/{cid}"> one-hop link href are load-bearing anti-merging + drill-link logic; mutation-test the one-flag-per-cid branch (DISTINCT HashSet membership collapse) and the link href construction so a mutant emitting a 'countered by N' count, a verdict, or a non-anchor control is killed.

steps.name: A large multi-pairing/multi-contribution /score breakdown flags every countered contribution correctly in one request (N+1-flatten behavioral proxy, REAL work + adapter-duckdb single-query note)
steps.description: The N+1-flatten behavioral proxy on the score surface (US-CF-001, SF-N1; AC-001-ONE-CALL / AC-001-INVARIANT / ADR-051). A LARGE /score breakdown with MANY pairings × MANY contributions and a KNOWN countered subset flags EVERY countered contribution correctly — and only those — in ONE request, with no per-pairing/per-contribution degradation. This is the at-this-layer behavioral proxy for the single flattened presence call (the score_counter_presence helper collects every Contribution.cid across every WeightedPairing from view.ranked.flat_map(contributions) and queries ONCE, ADR-051 / DD-14-2); if the presence read were per-pairing or per-contribution, a large multi-pairing breakdown would degrade or mis-flag under the fan-out, so this proxy pins the whole breakdown is flagged correctly in one shot. REAL work: requires seed_score_breakdown_many_pairings_known_countered_subset to seed a genuinely large multi-pairing breakdown with a real countered subset AND un-countered contributions (a MIXED breakdown) so the proxy is meaningful (the AT pins both subsets non-empty so the seed cannot silently shrink). The render's ranking/order + every number stays byte-identical to slice-09 even at this size (asserted via assert_score_render_byte_identical_to_slice09 over seeded.ordered_cids). ADAPTER NOTE for DELIVER: the strict 1-query bound is NOT observable at the subprocess AT layer — confirm the REUSED slice-12 adapter-duckdb counter_presence_for single-aggregate IN(...) read already carries its N+1 unit/property assertion (inherited from slice-12 / ADR-048); only add a fresh adapter-duckdb assertion if the flattened multi-pairing contribution-CID set exercises a path the slice-12 test does not already cover (query count invariant to pairing/contribution count). Build-before-run as 01-01. xtask delta: NONE.
criteria: GET /score?contributor=<did> over a LARGE multi-pairing breakdown (seed_score_breakdown_many_pairings_known_countered_subset, a MIXED breakdown with a non-empty countered subset AND un-countered contributions) flags EVERY countered contribution (assert_score_row_flagged_countered for every countered cid) and flags NO un-countered contribution (assert_score_row_not_flagged for every un-countered cid) — all in the ONE response, with no per-pairing/per-contribution degradation (AC-001-ONE-CALL).

The whole multi-pairing breakdown is flagged correctly from a single GET request, exercising the ADR-051 single flattened score_counter_presence call over the union of EVERY Contribution.cid across EVERY WeightedPairing (the subprocess-layer behavioral proxy for the no-N+1 guarantee); the ranking/row order + every number stays byte-identical to slice-09 even at this size (assert_score_render_byte_identical_to_slice09 over seeded.ordered_cids, AC-001-INVARIANT).

DELIVER confirms the strict single-query bound is covered by the inherited slice-12 adapter-duckdb counter_presence_for unit/property assertion (query count invariant to input size, ADR-048), adding a fresh adapter-duckdb assertion ONLY if the flattened multi-pairing contribution-CID set exercises an uncovered path; the AT in this step is the behavioral proxy, not the strict bound.
acceptance_criteria: GET /score?contributor=<did> over a LARGE multi-pairing breakdown (seed_score_breakdown_many_pairings_known_countered_subset, a MIXED breakdown with a non-empty countered subset AND un-countered contributions) flags EVERY countered contribution (assert_score_row_flagged_countered for every countered cid) and flags NO un-countered contribution (assert_score_row_not_flagged for every un-countered cid) — all in the ONE response, with no per-pairing/per-contribution degradation (AC-001-ONE-CALL).

The whole multi-pairing breakdown is flagged correctly from a single GET request, exercising the ADR-051 single flattened score_counter_presence call over the union of EVERY Contribution.cid across EVERY WeightedPairing (the subprocess-layer behavioral proxy for the no-N+1 guarantee); the ranking/row order + every number stays byte-identical to slice-09 even at this size (assert_score_render_byte_identical_to_slice09 over seeded.ordered_cids, AC-001-INVARIANT).

DELIVER confirms the strict single-query bound is covered by the inherited slice-12 adapter-duckdb counter_presence_for unit/property assertion (query count invariant to input size, ADR-048), adding a fresh adapter-duckdb assertion ONLY if the flattened multi-pairing contribution-CID set exercises an uncovered path; the AT in this step is the behavioral proxy, not the strict bound.
steps.mutation_note: The single flattened contribution-CID score_counter_presence call across all pairings (the no-N+1 guarantee) is the load-bearing performance contract; mutation-test the flatten (every pairing's contribution CID included in the one bound set) + the inherited adapter-duckdb single-query bound so a mutant that fans the read out per pairing/per contribution or drops a CID from the flattened set is killed.

phases.name: Scoring semantics — the CARDINAL sum-to-weight + byte-identity orthogonality, the anti-misread copy, the no-noise baseline, and htmx/no-JS parity on the flagged /score surface (mostly confirmatory once Phase 01 holds)

steps.name: On a FLAGGED breakdown the per-contribution subtotals STILL sum to the displayed pairing weight and the countered contribution renders its FULL original subtotal (the CARDINAL sum-to-weight, the counter subtracts nothing)
steps.description: The slice-14 CARDINAL sum-to-weight on a FLAGGED breakdown (US-CF-002, SF-3; AC-SCORE-SUMWEIGHT). Confirmatory once 01-01 exists — the render projects the SAME unchanged WeightedPairing and the presence set only gates an additive marker, so the orthogonality holds by construction (ADR-051 §7); this step PINS it on the observable flagged surface. On a breakdown WHERE one contribution carries the 'Countered' marker, the displayed per-contribution subtotals STILL sum to the displayed pairing weight, and the countered contribution's subtotal is its FULL original value (the counter subtracts nothing — SHOWN, never APPLIED). Reuses/adapts the slice-09 reproduce-by-hand parser on the FLAGGED render (markers elided so the subtotal parse is unaffected) via assert_score_html_breakdown_sums_to_weight_with_flag, plus assert_score_legend_present_and_blocklist_clean for the legend. Reuses seed_score_breakdown_one_contribution_countered. Build-before-run as 01-01. xtask delta: NONE.
criteria: GET /score?contributor=<did> over seed_score_breakdown_one_contribution_countered renders the countered contribution row WITH its 'Countered' marker (the breakdown IS flagged; assert_score_row_flagged_countered for every countered cid), yet the displayed per-contribution subtotals STILL sum to the displayed pairing weight (assert_score_html_breakdown_sums_to_weight_with_flag) — the slice-14 CARDINAL: the reproduce-by-hand sum-to-weight gate holds on a FLAGGED render (AC-SCORE-SUMWEIGHT).

The countered contribution's subtotal is its FULL original value — the counter subtracts nothing; there is no code path by which the presence set reaches a weight/subtotal/base/bonus/bucket/rank/order (the flag is additive markup BESIDE the verbatim subtotal, ADR-051 §7).

The breakdown carries the SCORE_COUNTER_LEGEND anti-misread legend (the breakdown is scored + flagged; assert_score_legend_present_and_blocklist_clean) and the assertion enters through the real openlore ui subprocess + HTTP, asserting on the observable rendered HTML only.
acceptance_criteria: GET /score?contributor=<did> over seed_score_breakdown_one_contribution_countered renders the countered contribution row WITH its 'Countered' marker (the breakdown IS flagged; assert_score_row_flagged_countered for every countered cid), yet the displayed per-contribution subtotals STILL sum to the displayed pairing weight (assert_score_html_breakdown_sums_to_weight_with_flag) — the slice-14 CARDINAL: the reproduce-by-hand sum-to-weight gate holds on a FLAGGED render (AC-SCORE-SUMWEIGHT).

The countered contribution's subtotal is its FULL original value — the counter subtracts nothing; there is no code path by which the presence set reaches a weight/subtotal/base/bonus/bucket/rank/order (the flag is additive markup BESIDE the verbatim subtotal, ADR-051 §7).

The breakdown carries the SCORE_COUNTER_LEGEND anti-misread legend (the breakdown is scored + flagged; assert_score_legend_present_and_blocklist_clean) and the assertion enters through the real openlore ui subprocess + HTTP, asserting on the observable rendered HTML only.
steps.mutation_note: The sum-to-weight-on-a-flagged-render orthogonality (the countered contribution keeps its FULL subtotal; the presence bool never reaches a number) is the slice-14 CARDINAL; mutation-test that no presence-set value can perturb a subtotal/weight/bonus/bucket/rank/order so a mutant that subtracts on counter, scales a subtotal, or feeds presence into the weight is killed.

steps.name: Adding the score flag changes NO weight/ranking/row order versus slice-09 — with markers + legend elided the /score render is byte-identical to the slice-09 baseline (the CARDINAL byte-identity, marker+legend elision)
steps.description: The CARDINAL byte-identity vs the slice-09 baseline (US-CF-002, SF-4; AC-SCORE-BYTEID / I-CF-9). Confirmatory once 01-01 exists (the rows are built from the UNCHANGED scoring::score output and the flag + legend are separate additive markup), but it is the load-bearing shown-never-applied gate on the score numbers. Adding the flag changes NO weight, ranking, or row order versus the slice-09 baseline: with the 'Countered' markers AND the SCORE_COUNTER_LEGEND elided, the /score render is byte-identical to slice-09 — every displayed weight, confidence, author bonus, triangulation bonus, subtotal, headline total, bucket, [SPARSE] line, the pairing ranking, and the contribution row order. Uses the slice-12/13 baseline+marker-elision tactic EXTENDED to also elide the legend: there is NO pre-flag binary and NO no-flag HTTP seam (the route ALWAYS reads counter_presence_for), so the slice-09 reference is the RECORDED ranked render order (the seed's ordered_cids), and the gold ELIDES the additive Countered anchors AND the legend, proving the remaining slice-09 body honours that recorded ranking/order + every number byte-for-byte (assert_score_render_byte_identical_to_slice09). Reuses seed_score_breakdown_many_pairings_known_countered_subset (a MIXED breakdown so the elision is non-trivial). Build-before-run as 01-01. xtask delta: NONE.
criteria: GET /score?contributor=<did> over seed_score_breakdown_many_pairings_known_countered_subset renders exactly the countered contributions WITH the marker (assert_score_row_flagged_countered) and every un-countered contribution exactly as slice-09 (assert_score_row_not_flagged), and with the additive 'Countered' markers AND the SCORE_COUNTER_LEGEND elided the render is byte-identical to the recorded slice-09 baseline — every weight/confidence/author-bonus/triangulation-bonus/subtotal/headline-total/bucket/[SPARSE]-line/pairing-ranking/contribution-row-order unchanged (assert_score_render_byte_identical_to_slice09 over seeded.ordered_cids, AC-SCORE-BYTEID).

The baseline is the RECORDED slice-09 ranked render order (ordered_cids) of the SAME store, since there is no pre-flag binary and no no-flag HTTP seam (the route ALWAYS reads counter_presence_for); the gold elides the Countered anchors AND the legend and proves the remaining body honours that recorded ranking/order byte-for-byte (the slice-12/13 baseline+marker-elision tactic extended to the legend).
Any divergence is an UNSHIPPABLE shown-never-applied breach (I-CF-9); the assertion enters through the real openlore ui subprocess + HTTP and asserts on the observable rendered HTML only.
acceptance_criteria: GET /score?contributor=<did> over seed_score_breakdown_many_pairings_known_countered_subset renders exactly the countered contributions WITH the marker (assert_score_row_flagged_countered) and every un-countered contribution exactly as slice-09 (assert_score_row_not_flagged), and with the additive 'Countered' markers AND the SCORE_COUNTER_LEGEND elided the render is byte-identical to the recorded slice-09 baseline — every weight/confidence/author-bonus/triangulation-bonus/subtotal/headline-total/bucket/[SPARSE]-line/pairing-ranking/contribution-row-order unchanged (assert_score_render_byte_identical_to_slice09 over seeded.ordered_cids, AC-SCORE-BYTEID).

The baseline is the RECORDED slice-09 ranked render order (ordered_cids) of the SAME store, since there is no pre-flag binary and no no-flag HTTP seam (the route ALWAYS reads counter_presence_for); the gold elides the Countered anchors AND the legend and proves the remaining body honours that recorded ranking/order byte-for-byte (the slice-12/13 baseline+marker-elision tactic extended to the legend).
Any divergence is an UNSHIPPABLE shown-never-applied breach (I-CF-9); the assertion enters through the real openlore ui subprocess + HTTP and asserts on the observable rendered HTML only.
steps.mutation_note: The byte-identity-with-markers+legend-elided gate (the flag never perturbs a number, re-ranks a pairing, or re-orders a contribution row) is load-bearing shown-never-applied logic; mutation-test the byte-identity across flagged/elided so a mutant that lets the flag or legend perturb a subtotal/total/bucket/[SPARSE]-line, re-rank, or re-order is killed.

steps.name: Two contributions with identical confidence+bonuses render IDENTICAL subtotals — only the countered one shows the marker, the legend is present, and the copy never uses a verdict/penalty word (anti-misread)
steps.description: The anti-misread copy on identical-subtotal twins (US-CF-002, SF-5; AC-SCORE-ANTIMISREAD). The flag is shown for the reader to judge and is unmistakably orthogonal to the score. Two contributions in the SAME pairing with IDENTICAL confidence + bonuses render IDENTICAL subtotals — one countered, one not — and only the countered one shows the marker; the breakdown carries the plain-language SCORE_COUNTER_LEGEND; the WHOLE rendered body is blocklist-clean (NEVER disputed/refuted/false/penalty/deduction/lowered/disputed score). The identical-subtotal twin proves the counter subtracts nothing: the countered contribution and its twin fold into the same displayed weight (the twin lives in uncountered_cids, so the per-row subtotals are equal). Reuses assert_score_row_flagged_countered / assert_score_row_not_flagged / assert_score_html_breakdown_sums_to_weight_with_flag / assert_score_legend_present_and_blocklist_clean. Materializes seed_score_breakdown_identical_subtotals_one_countered in support/mod.rs. Confirmatory once 01-01 + 02-01 hold. Build-before-run as 01-01. xtask delta: NONE.
criteria: GET /score?contributor=<did> over seed_score_breakdown_identical_subtotals_one_countered (two contributions in the same pairing with identical confidence + bonuses, one countered, one not) shows ONLY the countered one with the 'Countered' marker (assert_score_row_flagged_countered) and its identical-subtotal twin none (assert_score_row_not_flagged), proving the marker is orthogonal to the number.

Both contributions render the IDENTICAL subtotal (the counter subtracts nothing) and the subtotals STILL sum to the displayed pairing weight (assert_score_html_breakdown_sums_to_weight_with_flag) — the countered contribution kept its FULL original value, its identical-subtotal twin's subtotal is equal, and both fold into the same displayed weight.

The breakdown carries the plain-language SCORE_COUNTER_LEGEND anti-misread legend and the WHOLE rendered body is blocklist-clean — never disputed/refuted/false/penalty/deduction/lowered/disputed score (assert_score_legend_present_and_blocklist_clean, AC-SCORE-ANTIMISREAD).
acceptance_criteria: GET /score?contributor=<did> over seed_score_breakdown_identical_subtotals_one_countered (two contributions in the same pairing with identical confidence + bonuses, one countered, one not) shows ONLY the countered one with the 'Countered' marker (assert_score_row_flagged_countered) and its identical-subtotal twin none (assert_score_row_not_flagged), proving the marker is orthogonal to the number.

Both contributions render the IDENTICAL subtotal (the counter subtracts nothing) and the subtotals STILL sum to the displayed pairing weight (assert_score_html_breakdown_sums_to_weight_with_flag) — the countered contribution kept its FULL original value, its identical-subtotal twin's subtotal is equal, and both fold into the same displayed weight.

The breakdown carries the plain-language SCORE_COUNTER_LEGEND anti-misread legend and the WHOLE rendered body is blocklist-clean — never disputed/refuted/false/penalty/deduction/lowered/disputed score (assert_score_legend_present_and_blocklist_clean, AC-SCORE-ANTIMISREAD).
steps.mutation_note: The identical-subtotal-twins render (equal subtotals, only the countered one flagged) + the blocklist-clean legend copy are load-bearing anti-misread logic; mutation-test the legend blocklist scan and the twin-subtotal equality so a mutant emitting a verdict/penalty word, perturbing the countered twin's subtotal, or flagging the un-countered twin is killed.

steps.name: A contributor with NO countered contributions renders /score with no markers, no noise, byte-identical to the slice-09 baseline (no-noise, empty presence set)
steps.description: The no-noise + byte-identity baseline on the score surface (US-CF-002, SF-6; AC-002-NO-NOISE / AC-SCORE-BYTEID). A contributor with NO countered contributions: counter_presence_for returns the EMPTY set → no contribution row is flagged → every contribution renders exactly as slice-09, no 'Countered' marker anywhere AND no empty-state / '0 counters' noise, and (since there is nothing to flag) the breakdown is byte-identical to the slice-09 baseline with the additive legend elided. This is the byte-unaffected guarantee for the common case (the no-noise half of the trust contract) on the scoring surface. Reuses assert_score_row_not_flagged + assert_score_render_byte_identical_to_slice09; materializes seed_score_breakdown_none_countered in support/mod.rs by seeding the contributor's scoring trail through the production paths with NO counter (every contribution CID uncountered, empty countered set). Confirmatory once 01-01 exists (the empty-set → no-flag branch already holds). Build-before-run as 01-01. xtask delta: NONE.
criteria: GET /score?contributor=<did> over seed_score_breakdown_none_countered renders EVERY contribution as in slice-09 with NO 'Countered' marker (assert_score_row_not_flagged for each contribution cid) and the flag anchor appears NOWHERE in the body (empty presence set → nothing rendered; AC-002-NO-NOISE).
The breakdown carries NO empty-state / '0 counters' noise anywhere — the empty presence set produces an additive-nothing render (the un-countered common case is byte-unaffected, AC-002-NO-NOISE).

With nothing flagged and the additive legend elided, the render is byte-identical to the slice-09 baseline (assert_score_render_byte_identical_to_slice09 over seeded.ordered_cids) — no flag re-ordered/re-ranked anything (AC-SCORE-BYTEID baseline); seed_score_breakdown_none_countered is materialized in support/mod.rs by seeding through the production paths (no hand-inserted rows).
acceptance_criteria: GET /score?contributor=<did> over seed_score_breakdown_none_countered renders EVERY contribution as in slice-09 with NO 'Countered' marker (assert_score_row_not_flagged for each contribution cid) and the flag anchor appears NOWHERE in the body (empty presence set → nothing rendered; AC-002-NO-NOISE).
The breakdown carries NO empty-state / '0 counters' noise anywhere — the empty presence set produces an additive-nothing render (the un-countered common case is byte-unaffected, AC-002-NO-NOISE).

With nothing flagged and the additive legend elided, the render is byte-identical to the slice-09 baseline (assert_score_render_byte_identical_to_slice09 over seeded.ordered_cids) — no flag re-ordered/re-ranked anything (AC-SCORE-BYTEID baseline); seed_score_breakdown_none_countered is materialized in support/mod.rs by seeding through the production paths (no hand-inserted rows).
steps.mutation_note: The empty-presence-set → no-flag / no-noise / legend-elided byte-identity branch is load-bearing; mutation-test the empty-set no-marker path so a mutant emitting an empty-state '0 counters' noise string or a spurious marker on an un-countered contribution is killed.

steps.name: The /score flag renders identically under htmx fragment and no-JS full page — both offline (parity by construction, the page embeds the same fragment fn)
steps.description: The htmx-fragment / no-JS full-page parity on the flagged score surface (US-CF-002, SF-7; AC-002-PARITY / AC-SCORE-LOCAL). GET /score?contributor=<did> WITH HX-Request serves the #score-results fragment WITH the flag; WITHOUT HX-Request serves a COMPLETE full page whose score region renders the SAME flags — parity by construction (the page EMBEDS the same render_score_results_fragment fn; the flag + legend live INSIDE the fragment, ADR-051 §6.1). Both render fully with NO network access (the presence read is a LOCAL indexed lookup; the page references only the vendored local /static/htmx.min.js). The full page is is_full_page() (chrome present); the fragment is is_fragment() (no chrome); BOTH carry the SAME 'Countered' marker on the countered contribution and the SAME legend, NEITHER flags an un-countered contribution. Reuses assert_score_row_flagged_countered / assert_score_row_not_flagged / assert_score_legend_present_and_blocklist_clean + seed_score_breakdown_one_contribution_countered. Confirmatory once 01-01 exists (the flag + legend live in the single fragment fn both shapes embed). Build-before-run as 01-01. xtask delta: NONE.
criteria: GET /score?contributor=<did> WITHOUT HX-Request returns a complete full page (is_full_page(), chrome present) and WITH HX-Request returns ONLY the #score-results fragment (is_fragment(), no chrome); BOTH are 200 text/html.

The countered contribution row carries the SAME 'Countered' marker in BOTH shapes (assert_score_row_flagged_countered on full + fragment) and every un-countered contribution row carries NONE in either (assert_score_row_not_flagged), and BOTH shapes carry the SCORE_COUNTER_LEGEND (assert_score_legend_present_and_blocklist_clean) — parity by construction (the full page EMBEDS the same render_score_results_fragment fn; AC-002-PARITY).

Both shapes render fully with NO network access — the presence read is a LOCAL indexed lookup and the page references only the vendored local /static/htmx.min.js (AC-SCORE-LOCAL); the assertion enters through the real openlore ui subprocess + HTTP.
acceptance_criteria: GET /score?contributor=<did> WITHOUT HX-Request returns a complete full page (is_full_page(), chrome present) and WITH HX-Request returns ONLY the #score-results fragment (is_fragment(), no chrome); BOTH are 200 text/html.

The countered contribution row carries the SAME 'Countered' marker in BOTH shapes (assert_score_row_flagged_countered on full + fragment) and every un-countered contribution row carries NONE in either (assert_score_row_not_flagged), and BOTH shapes carry the SCORE_COUNTER_LEGEND (assert_score_legend_present_and_blocklist_clean) — parity by construction (the full page EMBEDS the same render_score_results_fragment fn; AC-002-PARITY).

Both shapes render fully with NO network access — the presence read is a LOCAL indexed lookup and the page references only the vendored local /static/htmx.min.js (AC-SCORE-LOCAL); the assertion enters through the real openlore ui subprocess + HTTP.
steps.mutation_note: The full-page-embeds-fragment parity (the flag + legend live in the single render_score_results_fragment fn both shapes embed) is load-bearing; mutation-test the parity so a mutant that flags only one shape, drifts the marker/legend between shapes, or flags an un-countered contribution in either shape is killed.

phases.name: GOLD invariants — read-only / no-write + offline chrome / offline render + the CARDINAL sum-to-weight byte-identity + the N+1-flatten proxy over the WHOLE flagged /score surface (mostly confirmatory, release gates LAST)

steps.name: Every flagged /score render leaves the store read-only + no flagged /score render adds a write/sign/counter control (read-only / no-write gold)
steps.description: Two cross-cutting gold guardrails that tend to flip green for free once Phases 01-02 are done (US-CF-002, SF-INV-ReadOnly + SF-INV-NoWrite; I-CF-1 / KPI-VIEW-2). (ReadOnly) Exercising /score across postures (countered + un-countered) AND both shapes (full page + htmx fragment) leaves the claims + peer_claims row counts UNCHANGED, asserted via the inherited universe-bound assert_store_read_only over capture_store_row_count_universe (Mandate 8: the two port-exposed counts, all unchanged) over a NON-TRIVIAL seeded store — the REUSED batch presence read is a read-only SELECT and the score is computed per query, nothing persisted on ANY surface. (NoWrite) No /score response shape (full page or fragment, countered or not) renders a write / sign / counter / publish / subscribe control — authoring stays the slice-03 CLI; every '/claims/{cid}' reference is render-only <a href> navigation TEXT (the per-reference anchor scan: every '/claims/' byte sits inside an '<a href' tag). Reuses the inherited harnesses (assert_store_read_only, capture_store_row_count_universe, assert_score_html_has_no_write_or_sign_control — the slice-09 score no-write blocklist) + seed_score_breakdown_one_contribution_countered. Lands in viewer_counter_flags_score_surface_invariants.rs. Build-before-run as 01-01. xtask delta: NONE.
criteria: Exercising /score (countered posture, full page + htmx fragment) over a non-trivial seeded store (seed_score_breakdown_one_contribution_countered) leaves the claims and peer_claims row counts identical (assert_store_read_only over capture_store_row_count_universe, all slots unchanged) — any change is an UNSHIPPABLE write-surface breach (I-CF-1 / KPI-VIEW-2); the REUSED batch presence read is a read-only SELECT and the score is computed per query, nothing persisted on any surface.

No /score response shape (full page or fragment, countered or un-countered) renders a write / sign / counter / publish / subscribe control (assert_score_html_has_no_write_or_sign_control), and every '/claims/{cid}' reference is render-only navigation TEXT inside an <a href> anchor, never an executable control (the per-reference anchor scan: every '/claims/' byte sits inside an '<a href' tag; I-CF-1).
Both guardrails enter through the real openlore ui subprocess + HTTP and assert on the observable rendered HTML / the port-exposed row-count universe only.
acceptance_criteria: Exercising /score (countered posture, full page + htmx fragment) over a non-trivial seeded store (seed_score_breakdown_one_contribution_countered) leaves the claims and peer_claims row counts identical (assert_store_read_only over capture_store_row_count_universe, all slots unchanged) — any change is an UNSHIPPABLE write-surface breach (I-CF-1 / KPI-VIEW-2); the REUSED batch presence read is a read-only SELECT and the score is computed per query, nothing persisted on any surface.

No /score response shape (full page or fragment, countered or un-countered) renders a write / sign / counter / publish / subscribe control (assert_score_html_has_no_write_or_sign_control), and every '/claims/{cid}' reference is render-only navigation TEXT inside an <a href> anchor, never an executable control (the per-reference anchor scan: every '/claims/' byte sits inside an '<a href' tag; I-CF-1).
Both guardrails enter through the real openlore ui subprocess + HTTP and assert on the observable rendered HTML / the port-exposed row-count universe only.

steps.name: The flagged /score chrome stays offline (no CDN) and the flagged /score surface renders fully offline (offline gold)
steps.description: Two offline gold guardrails (US-CF-002, SF-INV-OfflineChrome + SF-INV-Offline; I-CF-5 / KPI-HX-G2 / KPI-5 / AC-SCORE-LOCAL). (OfflineChrome) the flagged /score full page references ONLY the LOCAL /static/htmx.min.js script src and NO off-host CDN (references_external_cdn() is false), so the page CHROME — and, since the presence read is LOCAL, the FLAG itself — stays offline-capable. (Offline) the flagged /score surface renders fully with NO network seam wired (plain ViewerServer::start, NEITHER the /scrape GitHub seam NOR the /search indexer seam) over a store seeded with a contribution countered by a PULLED PEER (verified at peer pull time) — the presence read is a LOCAL DB-index lookup + the score is a LOCAL feed read + PURE compute with NO outbound edge, so the countered contribution STILL carries its marker with NO degraded notice (none of 'unavailable', 'network error', 'could not reach', 'try again'; the viewer re-verifies nothing). Reuses references_external_cdn + assert_score_row_flagged_countered + seed_score_breakdown_one_contribution_countered. Lands in viewer_counter_flags_score_surface_invariants.rs; flips green for free once 01-01 + the chrome exist. Build-before-run as 01-01. xtask delta: NONE.
criteria: The flagged /score full page references NO off-host CDN (references_external_cdn() is false — only the local /static/htmx.min.js, I-CF-5 / KPI-HX-G2), so the page chrome and (since the presence read is LOCAL) the flag itself stay offline-capable.

GET /score?contributor=<did> over a store with a contribution countered by a PULLED PEER, with NO network seam wired (plain ViewerServer::start), renders the countered contribution STILL carrying the 'Countered' marker from the LOCAL store with no network call (assert_score_row_flagged_countered; the presence read is a LOCAL ref-tables-only lookup + the score is a LOCAL compute, AC-SCORE-LOCAL).

The surface renders NO network-degraded notice (none of 'unavailable', 'network error', 'could not reach', 'try again') — the flagged /score route has no outbound edge to take down; the viewer re-verifies nothing (I-CF-5 / KPI-5).
acceptance_criteria: The flagged /score full page references NO off-host CDN (references_external_cdn() is false — only the local /static/htmx.min.js, I-CF-5 / KPI-HX-G2), so the page chrome and (since the presence read is LOCAL) the flag itself stay offline-capable.

GET /score?contributor=<did> over a store with a contribution countered by a PULLED PEER, with NO network seam wired (plain ViewerServer::start), renders the countered contribution STILL carrying the 'Countered' marker from the LOCAL store with no network call (assert_score_row_flagged_countered; the presence read is a LOCAL ref-tables-only lookup + the score is a LOCAL compute, AC-SCORE-LOCAL).

The surface renders NO network-degraded notice (none of 'unavailable', 'network error', 'could not reach', 'try again') — the flagged /score route has no outbound edge to take down; the viewer re-verifies nothing (I-CF-5 / KPI-5).

steps.name: CARDINAL byte-identity GOLD — the /score render is byte-identical with and without the flag (markers + legend elided) AND the subtotals still sum to the weight on a FLAGGED render + the N+1-flatten proxy resolves presence in one request (LAST, release gate)
steps.description: The CARDINAL shown-never-applied / sum-to-weight byte-identity gold + the N+1-flatten behavioral proxy (US-CF-001/002, SF-INV-ByteId + SF-INV-N1; I-CF-9 / D-14-2 / AC-SCORE-BYTEID + AC-SCORE-SUMWEIGHT + I-CF-8 / ADR-051; release-blocking). (ByteId, THE slice-14 CARDINAL) the SAME store's rendered breakdown — every weight, confidence, author bonus, triangulation bonus, subtotal, headline total, bucket, [SPARSE] line, pairing ranking, and contribution row order — is byte-IDENTICAL whether or not the flag is active (the additive 'Countered' markers AND the SCORE_COUNTER_LEGEND elided), AND the per-contribution subtotals STILL sum to the displayed pairing weight on the FLAGGED render. BASELINE TACTIC (the slice-12/13 recorded-ordering + marker-elision tactic extended to the legend): there is NO pre-flag binary and NO no-flag HTTP seam (the route ALWAYS reads counter_presence_for), so the slice-09 reference is the RECORDED ranked render order (the seed's ordered_cids), and the gold ELIDES the additive anchors AND the legend and proves the remaining slice-09 body honours that recorded ranking/order + every number byte-for-byte (assert_score_render_byte_identical_to_slice09 + assert_score_html_breakdown_sums_to_weight_with_flag) over a MIXED countered + un-countered subset. A byte-identity / sum breach silently lets the flag perturb a subtotal or re-rank a pairing; this gold makes it unshippable. (N1) a LARGE multi-pairing breakdown flags the countered subset correctly in ONE request — the contribution-CID flatten across pairings is a single presence call (the subprocess-layer behavioral proxy; the strict 1-query bound is the DELIVER adapter-duckdb unit/property test). LAST roadmap step (tends to flip green for free once 01-01 + 02-02 hold, because the rows are built from the UNCHANGED scoring::score output and the flag + legend are separate additive markup, but it is the release gate). Reuses seed_score_breakdown_many_pairings_known_countered_subset + assert_score_render_byte_identical_to_slice09 + assert_score_html_breakdown_sums_to_weight_with_flag + assert_score_row_flagged_countered / assert_score_row_not_flagged. Lands in viewer_counter_flags_score_surface_invariants.rs. Build-before-run as 01-01. xtask delta: NONE.
criteria: GET /score?contributor=<did> over seed_score_breakdown_many_pairings_known_countered_subset (a MIX of countered + un-countered contributions across pairings) renders, with the additive 'Countered' markers AND the SCORE_COUNTER_LEGEND elided, a render byte-IDENTICAL to the recorded slice-09 baseline — every weight / confidence / author-bonus / triangulation-bonus / subtotal / headline-total / bucket / [SPARSE]-line / pairing-ranking / contribution-row-order unchanged (assert_score_render_byte_identical_to_slice09 over seeded.ordered_cids) AND the per-contribution subtotals STILL sum to the displayed pairing weight on the FLAGGED render (assert_score_html_breakdown_sums_to_weight_with_flag) — the flag is additive ONLY (I-CF-9 / AC-SCORE-BYTEID + AC-SCORE-SUMWEIGHT); any divergence is an UNSHIPPABLE release-blocking shown-never-applied breach.

A LARGE multi-pairing breakdown flags EVERY countered contribution (assert_score_row_flagged_countered) and NO un-countered contribution (assert_score_row_not_flagged) in ONE request — the at-this-layer behavioral proxy for the ADR-051 single flattened score_counter_presence call over the union of EVERY Contribution.cid across EVERY WeightedPairing (the strict 1-query bound is the inherited DELIVER adapter-duckdb unit/property test; query count is not observable at the subprocess AT layer, I-CF-8).

Both gold guardrails enter through the real openlore ui subprocess + HTTP and assert on the observable rendered HTML only; the baseline is the RECORDED slice-09 ranked order (ordered_cids) of the SAME store since there is no pre-flag binary and no no-flag HTTP seam (the slice-12/13 baseline+marker-elision tactic extended to also elide the legend).
acceptance_criteria: GET /score?contributor=<did> over seed_score_breakdown_many_pairings_known_countered_subset (a MIX of countered + un-countered contributions across pairings) renders, with the additive 'Countered' markers AND the SCORE_COUNTER_LEGEND elided, a render byte-IDENTICAL to the recorded slice-09 baseline — every weight / confidence / author-bonus / triangulation-bonus / subtotal / headline-total / bucket / [SPARSE]-line / pairing-ranking / contribution-row-order unchanged (assert_score_render_byte_identical_to_slice09 over seeded.ordered_cids) AND the per-contribution subtotals STILL sum to the displayed pairing weight on the FLAGGED render (assert_score_html_breakdown_sums_to_weight_with_flag) — the flag is additive ONLY (I-CF-9 / AC-SCORE-BYTEID + AC-SCORE-SUMWEIGHT); any divergence is an UNSHIPPABLE release-blocking shown-never-applied breach.

A LARGE multi-pairing breakdown flags EVERY countered contribution (assert_score_row_flagged_countered) and NO un-countered contribution (assert_score_row_not_flagged) in ONE request — the at-this-layer behavioral proxy for the ADR-051 single flattened score_counter_presence call over the union of EVERY Contribution.cid across EVERY WeightedPairing (the strict 1-query bound is the inherited DELIVER adapter-duckdb unit/property test; query count is not observable at the subprocess AT layer, I-CF-8).

Both gold guardrails enter through the real openlore ui subprocess + HTTP and assert on the observable rendered HTML only; the baseline is the RECORDED slice-09 ranked order (ordered_cids) of the SAME store since there is no pre-flag binary and no no-flag HTTP seam (the slice-12/13 baseline+marker-elision tactic extended to also elide the legend).
steps.mutation_note: The CARDINAL release-blocking byte-identity + sum-to-weight gate (the flag never perturbs a number, re-ranks a pairing, re-orders a contribution row, or subtracts on counter) + the single flattened contribution-CID presence call across all pairings (the no-N+1 guarantee). Mutation-test the byte-identity across flagged/elided + the sum-to-weight on a flagged render + the flatten (every pairing's contribution CID in the one bound set) — a mutant that feeds the presence bool into a subtotal/weight/rank/order or fans the read out per pairing/per contribution must be killed.

validation.note: APPROVED — all 6 dimensions PASS; 11 steps / 14 scenarios; two CARDINALs provable by construction
